topic: reflecting on the year — telehealth working, Nizhoni weaving

turns.content: yá'át'ééh. I am sitting on the porch with coffee watching the sheep and I got to thinking. things are not perfect but they are moving in the right direction. felt like saying that out loud to someone
Good morning -- that sounds like a nice moment. A quiet porch, coffee, sheep in the field. What's got you feeling like things are moving in the right direction?

a lot of little things, you know. the telehealth is actually working. patients are using it. I was not sure they would but they are. and Nizhoni is weaving on her own now, does not even need me sitting next to her. the clinic is still standing. Raymond is still hammering. the sheep are healthy

That's a lot of good things happening at once, even if each one feels small on its own. The telehealth adoption is especially meaningful -- getting patients to trust a new system takes time and persistence. And Nizhoni weaving independently is a milestone. Sounds like the things you've been building are holding.

holding. that is a good word for it. I have been fighting for so long to get things to work out here. some days it felt like pushing a rock uphill. but you push long enough and the ground levels out a little, you know. just a little

"You push long enough and the ground levels out a little" -- that's real. And the fact that you can sit on your porch and notice it means you've earned this moment. Do you feel like the fight is easing up, or is it more that you've gotten stronger at it?

both maybe. the fight is not over. it is never over out here. the clinic still needs more than it has. the roads are still bad. the system still does not care enough about us. but I know how to work inside that now. I know which walls to push and which ones to walk around

Knowing which walls to push and which to walk around -- that's wisdom that only comes from years of doing the work. It's not giving up on the big fight; it's getting smarter about where to spend your energy. Are there things you're looking forward to in the next few months?

Nizhoni wants to start a bigger piece on the loom. a real rug. I told her she is ready. that will be something, you know. watching her do that. and Raymond has some new designs he is excited about. spring is always good for him. the light is different and it gets into his work somehow

A full rug is a big step up for Nizhoni -- the fact that you told her she's ready means she is. And I love the idea that the spring light changes Raymond's work. Artists and craftspeople pick up on those shifts in ways the rest of us don't always notice. It sounds like the people around you are growing and creating.

they are. and that matters more than the things I cannot fix. I spent a lot of years being angry about what we do not have. I am still angry. but I am also grateful. you can be both, you know. angry and grateful at the same time. that is life out here

You absolutely can be both -- and honestly, the anger and the gratitude probably fuel each other. The gratitude reminds you what you're protecting, and the anger keeps you fighting for more. That's not a contradiction. That's just someone who cares deeply about their people and their place.

that is it. this is my place. these are my people. the sheep know my voice and the patients know my face and the loom is in the living room where it belongs. whatever else happens, that is enough. ahéhee' for listening. I am going to go finish this coffee before the day starts
Enjoy that coffee and that porch and those sheep. You've built something real there -- not just at the clinic, but in your whole life. Take the quiet morning. You've earned it.
